item.title: Nvidia, Arm Return the CPU to Center Stage in the Age of AI
item.description: The computing workhorse may be just as essential as it ever was

item.title: France Must Keep 2029 Deficit Target of 3% of GDP, Lescure Says
item.description: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said it’s imperative for the country to stick to its medium-term deficit-reduction target.

item.title: A ‘Fragile’ US Housing Market Heads to Spring Season Under War Strains
item.description: Mortgage rates are climbing again as traders price in faster inflation.

item.title: Austria Plans Social-Media Ban for Kids as It Awaits EU Rules
item.description: Austria plans to announce a sweeping ban on social media for children and add school classes on digital competency, moving ahead of the European Union’s efforts to protect kids from online addiction.

item.title: Dollar’s Surprise Gain Confounds Forecasters, Risks Adding to Inflation
item.description: Index of the greenback heads for its biggest monthly gain since July.

item.title: Microsoft Set for Worst Quarter Since 2008 as AI Takes Two Bites
item.description: Microsoft Corp. is at the intersection of two troubling trends roiling the technology sector, which has the stock on track for its worst quarterly performance since the global financial crisis two decades ago.

item.title: Blue Owl Pitches Family Offices While Navigating Market Turmoil
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc. is boosting its team dedicated to bringing the ultra-wealthy into private markets, betting investors will look past recent jitters to expand in an area where they’ve traditionally had limited exposure.

item.title: Oaktree Will Meet 8.5% Private Credit Fund Redemptions in Full
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management is meeting all redemption requests it received for a $7.7 billion private credit fund aimed at retail investors, siding with managers that have decided against enforcing a cap on withdrawals.

item.title: Iran Shifts Grain Imports to Gulf of Oman With Hormuz Locked Up
item.description: Iran appears to be shifting import routes to secure key grains, ensuring it can bring in staple food supplies while the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut.

item.title: The Startup Selling Full-Body Scans as the Future of Health Care
item.description: Step into the machine at Neko Health or one of its rivals to check for skin cancer, diabetes or stroke risk. Is this the next frontier in preventative health care, or something your primary care physician would rather you avoid?

item.title: Gates Adviser Served as Conduit to Epstein, Discussed ‘Nudes,’ ‘Hot Girls’
item.description: Tech mogul Bill Gates said he never went to Jeffrey Epstein’s island, where some of the late financier’s sex crimes occurred. But over a four-year span, Epstein made a concerted effort to lure him and members of his family to the Caribbean destination, emails show.

item.title: Canada Ex-Defense Minister’s Startup Juno Sees Anduril-Type Role
item.description: A defense technology startup chaired by Canada’s former defense minister is raising funds while the country rapidly rearms, and launched its first product aimed at Arctic security.

item.title: Economists See War Pushing US Inflation Above 3%, Hurting Growth
item.description: Economists raised their estimates for US inflation through year-end, while trimming consumer spending, growth and employment projections as the war in Iran drives up fuel costs.

item.title: Vital Oil Price Benchmarks Bent Out of Shape by Iran War
item.description: Asia’s oil refiners are seeking alternatives to the Middle East’s benchmark crude prices, as war-driven distortions fuel wild price swings that they say have come untethered from physical market realities.

item.title: Russia’s Key Baltic Oil Ports on Fire Again, Satellite Data Show
item.description: Russia’s crucial Baltic oil ports of Primorsk and Ust-Luga are on fire again after overnight drone attacks, according to data from NASA.

item.title: Europe’s Gas Dilemma Gets More Acute as Storage Season Starts
item.description: After a torrid month in which European gas prices surged, plunged and surged again on the war in the Middle East, traders now face a dilemma.

item.title: Japan’s Chinese Population Continues to Grow Amid Sour Ties
item.description: The number of Chinese residents in Japan has continued to rise, even as ties between the two countries have become increasingly fractious, according to data released Friday.

item.title: BOE Revamps Funding Tool for Banks Used Just Once Since 2008
item.description: The Bank of England has lowered the pricing on a funding tool designed to help banks withstand short-term liquidity shocks, a move that may boost the attractiveness of a facility only used once since its creation in 2008.

item.title: Odey Plays The Victim Card in Appeal to Overturn Finance Ban
item.description: After a long few weeks watching the UK’s financial watchdog present evidence that he had groped, verbally harassed and inappropriately touched female employees during his time at the helm of Odey Asset Management, former hedge fund boss Crispin Odey insisted to the court that he was the one who was actually the victim, and the target of “bullying” by the regulator.

item.title: Czech Premier Urges Fuel Retailers to Lower ‘Outrageous’ Prices
item.description: Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis urged two of the largest fuel distributors in the country to immediately lower prices at gas stations after the Middle East conflict triggered a surge in oil.

item.title: Russia Scraps Growth Downgrade as Iran War Boosts Oil Revenue
item.description: Russia is abandoning plans for a sharp downgrade to its 2026 growth forecast as the war in Iran boosts its oil revenue.

item.title: Two Chinese Container Ships Attempt Hormuz Exit Before U-Turning
item.description: Two large container ships linked to China’s state-owned Cosco Shipping Corp. attempted to exit the Persian Gulf on Friday through the Strait of Hormuz, before making an abrupt U-turn near Iran and motoring back.

item.title: Kenya Plans to Stabilize Fuel Price as Outages Hit Some Stations
item.description: Kenya plans to stabilize fuel prices as some stations run out of supply in the East African nation that typically depends on Middle East imports to meet demand.

item.title: China Retaliates With Trade Barrier Probes Against the US
item.description: China announced a pair of investigations into US practices concerning supply chains and renewable products, in retaliation of US trade probes ahead of an expected presidential summit.

item.title: Chip Hub Taiwan Keeps Power Prices Unchanged Despite War
item.description: Taiwan, home to some of the world’s biggest chipmakers, will keep its electricity rates unchanged for now to mitigate inflationary pressures from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ’s New Natural Rate Estimate Won’t Alter Economists’ Views
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s new estimate for the neutral rate of interest, a key indicator showing how much room authorities have to lift rates, was little changed from a previous projection, and therefore won’t likely prompt economists to alter their views on the policy trajectory.

item.title: Now There's a Helium Shortage and It Affects More Than Balloons
item.description: Another consequence of the war in Iran.

item.title: Odd Lots: The Helium Shortage Hurts More Than Balloons (Podcast)
item.description: Ripple effects from the war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz continue to widen. There’s yet another brewing shortage, this time in helium. While most people associate helium with balloons and funny voices, the element is used in a surprisingly wide variety of industrial settings, including semiconductor production, where its role in advanced lithography has been growing rapidly. But helium mining and exploration in North America has been practically non-existent for a variety of r

item.title: Spanish Prices Rise at Fastest Pace Since 2024 on Iran War
item.description: Spanish inflation jumped to its highest level since June 2024 due to the Iran war, supporting the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates.

item.title: Hong Kong Woos Central Banks in Bid to Become Gold-Trading Hub
item.description: Hong Kong is inviting a number of China-friendly central banks to participate in its gold-clearing system as part of a push to elevate the city as a major bullion-trading hub.
